quotes.topic: III7 VI7 II7 V7 progression intro
quotes.verbatim_quote:
The last harmonic formula we will deal with in this book is III7 VI7 117 V7. There are many different examples |
given because there are many different attractive sounding chords that fit together weil on this progression. These |
examples are organized, once again, according to which chord starts the progression.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Establishes chapter subject and organizational principle.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Occasionally, you may wish to try using iii7 types in place of IIJ7 types, or vi7 types in place of V17 types. In
many cases, either of these chords would sound very good — experiment and see what you think. Also, you might fool

around with IF or 17 for II]? {these are all common sounds),
quotes.why_load_bearing: Concrete substitution rules expanding harmonic vocabulary.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
almost all of the examples use altered dominant 7th
types (altered dominants are those with #9’s, 69’s, #5°s, and 65’s), which are very “spicy” chords.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Explicit definition of altered dominants by enumeration.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Take this
whole section in small doses and add some of your favorite examples of these IIJ7, VI7 LI7 ¥7’s to some of the more
“normal” progressions you have already studied; the ear will accept altered chords more easily in most cases if they are
prepared with sounds of a more normal, diatonic “major scaley’’ nature first.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Ear-training rule: prepare altered with diatonic.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
This type of technique (that is, of combining the consonant and dissonant types of progressions) is also fun for
your ear because of the contrast; and it is good for you because it will get you into the spirit of combining shorter
‘progressions in order to make longer progressions.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Compositional principle behind mixing diatonic and altered progressions.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Don’t forget to occasionally try resolving the V7’s to I’s.
TRY THIS CHORD IN PLACE OF A7b5 WHENEVER A7b5 PROGRESSES TO D7b9b5.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Contextual substitution tied to specific motion.

quotes.topic: Book must be studied in order
quotes.verbatim_quote:
If you decide to skip around, and you run across a chord that you cannot find the right fingering for, the chances
are great that the fingering was given earlier somewhere. Please remember, for your own good, this book is meant to be

approached in order, from the beginning to the end.
